name: Praga
description: Four-channel stereo VCA mixer with per-channel unipolar/bipolar CV modes, clickless mutes, two post-fader aux sends with stereo returns, direct VCA outs, and chainable sub-mix bus. Hrad expander adds master level and gate mutes.

controls.name: VOL
controls.description: Channel attenuator. Function depends on active volume mode — offset in unipolar, bipolar scaler in bipolar.
controls.detail: 0–10 · unipolar: fully open = 0dB at 8V CV · bipolar: fully CCW = -56dB, no modulation

controls.name: ACTIVE|MODE
controls.description: Illuminated button. Short press toggles clickless mute · long press switches volume control mode.
controls.detail: green = unipolar · red = bipolar · unlit = muted · mode-switch works while muted

controls.name: PAN
controls.description: Stereo panorama offset. Sums with PAN CV. 3dB equal-loudness pan law, compressed near 0dB to prevent loudness rise.
controls.detail: bipolar · CCW = L · noon = center · CW = R · CV expects ±5V

controls.name: AUX
controls.description: Bipolar send to aux A (CCW) or aux B (CW). Post-fader, derived from channel VCA output.
controls.detail: noon = no send · CCW = send A · CW = send B · post-fader only

controls.name: Post-fader LED meter
controls.description: Five-bar peak indicator referenced to 10Vpp. Thresholds -32 · -20 · -12 · -6 · 0dB.
controls.detail: top LED = clip warning · reads post-VOL, pre-pan/aux split

inputs.name: INPUT
inputs.description: Channel audio input. DC-coupled signal path accepts modular-level audio or CV.

inputs.name: PAN CV
inputs.description: Pan-position CV, summed with the PAN knob as offset.

inputs.name: VOL CV
inputs.description: Volume CV. Response differs per mode — unipolar for envelopes, bipolar for LFOs. Higher amplitudes tolerated but compressed above 0dB gain.

inputs.name: AUX RETURN A (L/R)
inputs.description: Stereo return pair for the aux A send. Summed directly into the main stereo bus.

inputs.name: AUX RETURN B (L/R)
inputs.description: Stereo return pair for the aux B send. Summed directly into the main stereo bus.

outputs.name: VCA OUT
outputs.description: Per-channel direct post-VCA tap, unaffected by PAN. Uses a separate VCA chip for a clean copy — intended for multitracking or insert-style routing via Hrad.

outputs.name: STEREO SUM L
outputs.description: Main mix bus, left. Soft-clipped above ~16Vpp to tame sum overs.

outputs.name: STEREO SUM R
outputs.description: Main mix bus, right. Soft-clipped above ~16Vpp to tame sum overs.

outputs.name: AUX SEND A
outputs.description: Mono post-fader send for bus A.

outputs.name: AUX SEND B
outputs.description: Mono post-fader send for bus B.

behaviors.name: Unipolar volume mode (envelope VCA)
behaviors.description: VOL knob biases the CV input. Full knob + 8V CV opens channel to 0dB, closed knob silences the channel to ~-85dB. Dynamic response to CV is preserved regardless of knob position — behaves like a CV-offset VCA. Above 0dB gain is soft-compressed (+3dB ceiling), giving a compressor-like taming rather than clipping.

behaviors.name: Bipolar volume mode (LFO amp mod)
behaviors.description: VOL knob scales the bipolar CV together with an internal offset. Depth of AM (in dB) shrinks as the knob closes — modulation is maximal only when the fader is up. Optimized for ±5V LFOs; +5V fully open = 0dB, fully closed ≈ -56dB steady. No clipping above 0dB; response compresses instead.

behaviors.name: Clickless mute / unmute
behaviors.description: Fades the channel to -90dB over a few milliseconds instead of hard-cutting. LED extinguishes while muted. Pressing again fades back in. Safe for performance muting under audio.

behaviors.name: Knob-as-offset with CV patched
behaviors.trigger: cable in VOL CV or PAN CV
behaviors.description: Knobs become offsets rather than primary controls when CV is patched. In unipolar mode, VOL still sets the floor of attenuation; in bipolar mode, it still scales depth. PAN knob biases the center point of CV-driven panning.

behaviors.name: Post-fader aux sends
behaviors.description: Sends tap the per-channel VCA output, so envelope/LFO amplitude modulation carries into the send effect. Channel mute also silences the send. AUX is bipolar with A on the CCW half and B on the CW half — noon is no send.

behaviors.name: Stereo aux returns route to main bus
behaviors.trigger: cable into AUX RETURN A or B
behaviors.description: Returns are stereo pairs summed directly into the main bus, not back through any channel. Aux A return shares its input jacks with the left pair, aux B with the right pair (see panel labels).

behaviors.name: Soft-clipped sum bus
behaviors.description: The stereo bus gently saturates when multiple hot sources sum. Prevents harsh hard-clipping. Manual recommends keeping faders 50–80% for clean headroom with typical modular levels.

behaviors.name: Chainable sub-mix via rear headers
behaviors.description: Multiple Pragas (or compatible mixers) cascade: each upstream unit injects its mix bus 1:1 into the next unit's bus, so the final Praga's STEREO SUM carries everything. IN of the first unit and OUT of the last remain open.

behaviors.name: Hrad expander integration
behaviors.description: Adds line-level outputs, master LEVEL with meter, headphones, cue input, gate inputs for automated muting, CV over the aux amounts, and channel insert points between VCA and aux selector.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled signal path
behaviors.description: All channels pass DC, so Praga doubles as a VCA/mixer for CV as well as audio — useful for summing envelopes, crossfading CV, or muting modulation sources.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice performance mixer with gated mutes
patchIdeas.patch: Four voices into INPUT 1–4 · each channel in unipolar (green) with envelope → VOL CV · manual mutes for live arrangement · sum via STEREO SUM to main out
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch2 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch3 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch4 Mode: UNIPOLAR

- Voice1 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 1)
- Env1 (Out) >> Praga (VOL CV 1)
- Voice2 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 2)
- Env2 (Out) >> Praga (VOL CV 2)
- Voice3 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 3)
- Voice4 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 4)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Tremolo on one channel via bipolar LFO
patchIdeas.patch: Switch Ch1 to bipolar (red) · LFO ±5V into VOL CV 1 · VOL knob sets tremolo depth (open = full depth, ~half = subtle) · other channels stay in unipolar
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1 Mode: BIPOLAR
* Ch1 VOL: 7 (depth)

- Pad (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 1)
- LFO (Sine) >> Praga (VOL CV 1)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Auto-panning pad
patchIdeas.patch: Pad into Ch2 · slow LFO ±5V → PAN CV 2 · PAN knob at noon for symmetric sweep · channel in unipolar, envelope shapes amplitude
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch2 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch2 PAN: noon

- Pad (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 2)
- AD Env (Out) >> Praga (VOL CV 2)
- Slow LFO (Tri) >> Praga (PAN CV 2)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Reverb send with pre-delay
patchIdeas.patch: Vocal/lead into Ch1 · AUX full CW to send B · AUX SEND B → reverb in · reverb L/R → AUX RETURN B pair · pre-delay via delay in the send path
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1 AUX: full CW (send B)

- Voice (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 1)
- Praga (AUX SEND B) -> Delay (In)
- Delay (Out) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (OutL) -> Praga (AUX RETURN B L)
- Reverb (OutR) -> Praga (AUX RETURN B R)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Parallel compression via dual aux loop
patchIdeas.patch: Drum bus into Ch3 · AUX full CW (B) → external compressor → AUX RETURN B · AUX full CCW (A) unused or to saturator · blend dry Ch3 with smashed parallel return
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch3 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch3 AUX: full CW

- Drums (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 3)
- Praga (AUX SEND B) -> Compressor (In)
- Compressor (OutL) -> Praga (AUX RETURN B L)
- Compressor (OutR) -> Praga (AUX RETURN B R)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Multitrack via VCA OUTs
patchIdeas.patch: Four voices in · record each VCA OUT to a separate DAW track (still VCA-shaped, no pan) · use STEREO SUM for headphone monitor mix with pan and aux fx
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1-4 Mode: UNIPOLAR

- Voice1 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 1)
- Voice2 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 2)
- Voice3 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 3)
- Voice4 (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 4)
- Praga (VCA OUT 1) -> Interface (Ch1)
- Praga (VCA OUT 2) -> Interface (Ch2)
- Praga (VCA OUT 3) -> Interface (Ch3)
- Praga (VCA OUT 4) -> Interface (Ch4)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: CV mixer for modulation summing
patchIdeas.patch: Use DC-coupled path to mix multiple CVs. Envelopes into INPUT 1–3 in unipolar mode, faders set weights · VCA OUT 1 (or STEREO SUM L) drives a filter cutoff
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch2 Mode: UNIPOLAR
* Ch3 Mode: UNIPOLAR

- EnvA (Out) >> Praga (INPUT 1)
- EnvB (Out) >> Praga (INPUT 2)
- LFO (Out) >> Praga (INPUT 3)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Cascaded 8-channel mixer (two Pragas)
patchIdeas.patch: Praga#1 channels 1–4, Praga#2 channels 5–8. 6-pin ribbon from Praga#1 OUT header → Praga#2 IN header. Praga#2 STEREO SUM carries all eight.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga#1:
* all channels: UNIPOLAR

Praga#2:
* all channels: UNIPOLAR

- Voice1 (Out) -> Praga#1 (INPUT 1)
- Voice2 (Out) -> Praga#1 (INPUT 2)
- Voice3 (Out) -> Praga#1 (INPUT 3)
- Voice4 (Out) -> Praga#1 (INPUT 4)
- Voice5 (Out) -> Praga#2 (INPUT 1)
- Voice6 (Out) -> Praga#2 (INPUT 2)
- Voice7 (Out) -> Praga#2 (INPUT 3)
- Voice8 (Out) -> Praga#2 (INPUT 4)
- Praga#2 (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga#2 (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Sidechain duck via bipolar mode
patchIdeas.patch: Bass on Ch1 unipolar · kick-triggered envelope inverted → VOL CV 1 in bipolar mode · knob sets duck floor · kick punches a hole in the bass
patchIdeas.diagram:
Praga:
* Ch1 Mode: BIPOLAR
* Ch1 VOL: 8

- Bass (Out) -> Praga (INPUT 1)
- Kick (Trig) t> Env (Trig)
- Env (Inv) >> Praga (VOL CV 1)
- Praga (STEREO SUM L) -> Output (L)
- Praga (STEREO SUM R) -> Output (R)
